{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Burundi women's national football team}}
{{Infobox National football team
| Name              = Burundi
| Badge             = 
| Nickname          = ''Intamba'' <br> ''Les Hirondelles'' <br> The Chayfs <br> (The [[Swallow]]s)
| Association       = [[Football Federation of Burundi|Fédération de Football du Burundi]] (FBF)<br>(Ishirahamwe ry'umupira w'amaguru ry'Uburundi)
| Sub-confederation = [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]] <br> (East & Central Africa)
| Confederation     = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach             = [[Saido Berahino]]
| Captain           = [[Frédéric Nsabiyumva]]
| Most caps         = [[Karim Nizigiyimana]] (66)
| Top scorer        = [[Fiston Abdul Razak]] (19)
| Home Stadium      = [[Intwari Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = BDI
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|BDI}}
| FIFA max          = 96
| FIFA max date     = August 1993
| FIFA min          = 160
| FIFA min date     = July 1998
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Burundi}}
| Elo max           = 89
| Elo max date      = 13 December 1998
| Elo min           = 168
| Elo min date      = 13 December 2019
 | pattern_la1      = _bdi25h
 | pattern_b1       = _bdi25h
 | pattern_ra1      = _bdi25h
 | pattern_sh1      = _bdi25h
 | pattern_so1      = _socks
 | leftarm1         = 1EC019
 | body1            = 1EC019
 | rightarm1        = 1EC019
 | shorts1          = 1EC019
 | socks1           = 1EC019
 | pattern_la2      = _bdi25a
 | pattern_b2       = _bdi25a
 | pattern_ra2      = _bdi25a
 | pattern_sh2      = _bdi25a
 | pattern_so2      = 
 | leftarm2         = ff0000
 | body2            = ff0000
 | rightarm2        = ff0000
 | shorts2          = ff0000
 | socks2           = ff0000
| First game         = {{fb|UGA}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|BDI|1962}} <br> ([[Kampala, Uganda]]; 9 October 1964)<ref name="Burundi RSSSF">{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesb/buru-intres.html |title=Burundi – List of International Matches |author=Barrie Courtney |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=2013-04-08 |archive-date=2022-11-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221129042742/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesb/buru-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
| Largest win        = {{fb|BDI}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|DJI}} <br> ([[Bujumbura, Burundi]]; 11 March 2017) <br> {{fb|BDI}} 8–1 {{fb-rt|Seychelles}}<br>([[Moroni, Comoros]]; 4 September 2021)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|CGO|1970}} 8–0 {{fb-rt|BDI|1967}} <br> ([[Cameroon]]; 24 December 1977)<ref name="Burundi RSSSF"/>
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 1
| Regional cup first = [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[African Nations Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 1
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Group stage ([[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]])
| 3rdRegional name      = [[CECAFA Cup]]
| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 14
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2004 CECAFA Cup|2004]])
|pattern_la3=_bdi25t|pattern_b3=_bdi25t|pattern_ra3=_bdi25t|pattern_sh3=_bdi25t|socks3=ffffff|shorts3=ffffff}}

The '''Burundi national football team''' ({{langx|rn|Umurwi nserukira gihugu w'umupira w'amaguru w'u Burundi }}), nicknamed ''The Fighting Swallows'' ({{langx|fr|Les Hirondelles en Guerre}}; {{langx|rn|Intamba m'Urugamba}}), represents [[Burundi]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Burundi]]. The team has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]]. Burundi previously did come very close to qualifying for the [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations]], losing only on penalties to [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] in a playoff. However, in [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]], it qualified for the first time in a major tournament, and took part in the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] finals in [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations Group B|Group B]], but lost all its matches and left from the group stage without scoring a single goal.

==History==
===Difficult beginnings (1974–1992)===
The Burundi football team was created in 1971 by the [[Football Federation of Burundi]]. The Swallows' first match was in a [[1976 African Cup of Nations qualification]] match against [[Somalia national football team|Somalia]], which ended in a 2–0 victory. Following a 1–0 loss in the second leg, Burundi qualified to meet Egypt in the next round where they lost 5–0 on aggregate and were eliminated. It would be seventeen years before Burundi played another AFCON qualifying match. In their first twenty years, Burundi played in twenty fixtures and managed 6 wins, 2 draws and 12 defeats.

===Narrowly missing qualification (1992–1998)===
In 1992, Burundi entered the qualification rounds for the FIFA World Cup for the first time, but were eliminated in the first round following one win (1–0 against [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]]), one draw (0–0 against [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]]) and two losses in the reverse fixtures to finish bottom of the group. Burundi then finished joint top in their [[1994 African Cup of Nations qualification]] group however they lost their play-off match against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] on penalties. Due to the [[Burundian Civil War]], Burundi withdrew from the 1996 and 1998 AFCON qualifiers and, despite beating [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] 2–0 on aggregate and qualifying for the final round of the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1998 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], they withdrew again.

===African Cup of Nations qualifiers (2000–2015)===
Following successive withdrawals from the AFCON qualifiers, Burundi returned for the [[2000 African Cup of Nations qualification|2000 competition]], beating [[Tanzania national football team|Tanzania]] in the preliminary rounds before finishing third in their group behind [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]] and [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]]. In the 2002 qualifiers, Burundi again advanced to the group stages of qualification by beating [[Djibouti national football team|Djibouti]] (4–1), but finished last in their group with only two points. In 2004, Burundi performed even worse, collecting no points and finishing last behind [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]] and [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]]. In 2008 Burundi finished five points behind leaders [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] and didn't make it through to the next round. In 2012 Burundi were even further adrift, finishing thirteen points behind group winners [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]]. For the 2013 qualifiers, Burundi failed to advance due to away goals against [[Zimbabwe national football team|Zimbabwe]] (2–2), and in 2015 they lost to [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]] (1–0) .

===FIFA World Cup qualifiers (2002–2014)===
Burundi did not participate in the 2002 qualification process but re-entered in 2006, only to get knocked out by [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]] in the first round (4–1). In 2010, Burundi managed two victories, both against [[Seychelles national football team|Seychelles]] but failed to go through behind [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]] and [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]]. The 2006 and 2010 qualifiers doubled as qualification for the FIFA World Cup and Africa Cup of Nations. The following qualification cycle, Burundi were eliminated by [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]] in the first round (3–2).

===First CAN qualification (2017–present)===

[[File:Saido Berahino 8886 (15632059695).jpg|thumb|200px|[[Saido Berahino]] scored on his international debut for Burundi in September 2018]]

After falling in the second qualifying round for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2018 FIFA World Cup]] against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]], Burundi focused on convincing [[Gaël Bigirimana]] and [[Saido Berahino]] to come and play for the selection, both playing in Europe (for [[Hibernian F.C.|Hibernian]] and [[Stoke City F.C.|Stoke City]] respectively). Both players agreed and Berahino scored on his debut to earn the team a 1–1 draw against [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]]. In March 2019, in the final group game, Burundi played a decisive match against Gabon needing only one point to qualify. The match ended in a draw (1–1), with a goal from [[Cédric Amissi]] earning Burundi their first opportunity to play in the Africa Cup of Nations. Burundi lost all three of their matches at the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations]], failing to register a single goal.

==Team image==
===Colours===
Traditionally, the Burundi team wears the three colours of its flag: red, green and white.

===Sponsors===
During these beginnings, Burundi is equipped by [[Erreà]], an Italian sports brand. In 2012, he signed a contract with [[Adidas]] for a period of 10 years. Despite this long-term contract with Adidas, Burundi is committed in 2018 with [[Nike, Inc.|Nike]]. She even signs a contract with [[Lumitel]] (a phone brand).

===Stadium===
The Burundi team have played most of their matches in [[Bujumbura]] at the [[Intwari Stadium]]. The second match in its history, Burundi is playing its first home match against Somalia (3–0 victory). The Intwari stadium is home to [[Vital'O FC]] and [[Prince Louis FC]], as well as the national team. The stadium has been renovated several times, notably in March 2018, due to a flooded pitch.

===Supporters===
During the matches at the [[Intwari Stadium]], the Burundian supporters used to be often outnumbered, in particular because of the rather low capacity of the stadium (10,000 seats) for around 13,000 spectators.  Just like the players, the supporters dress in red, green and white and bring the flag of the country. During the match in March 2019 against Gabon, the authorities have made additional seats available because of the lack of space in the stands.
In 2022, the stadium underwent renovation project increasing its capacity to 22,000 seats.

==Results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 5 September
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CIV}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
|goals1     =
*[[Vakoun Issouf Bayo|Bayo]] {{goal|3}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Felix Houphouet Boigny Stadium]]
|location   = [[Abidjan]], Ivory Coast
|attendance =
|referee    = [[Bouchra Karboubi]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020796 Report (FIFA)]<br>[https://www.cafonline.com/fifa-world-cup/match-centre?competition=340&season=112026&match=2407563 Report (CAF)]
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 9 September
|time       = {{UTZ|22:00|3}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
|goals1     =
*[[Alieu Fadera|Fadera]] {{goal|34|pen.}}
*[[Ebou Adams|Adams]] {{goal|76}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Nyayo National Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nairobi]], Kenya
|attendance =
|referee    = Mohamed Maarouf Eid Mansour ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020797 Report (FIFA)]<br>[https://www.cafonline.com/fifa-world-cup/match-centre?competition=340&season=112026&match=2407566 Report (CAF)]
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 14 October
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|KEN}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Ryan Ogam|Ogam]] {{goal|72}}
|stadium    = [[Intwari Stadium]]
|location   = [[Bujumbura]], Burundi
|attendance = 16,000
|referee    = Hillary Hambaba ([[Zimbabwe Football Association|Zimbabwe]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021063
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 14 October
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAB}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
|goals1     =
*[[Bryan Meyo|Meyo]] {{goal|86}}
*[[Mario Lemina|M. Lemina]] {{goal|90+1}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Stade de Franceville]]
|location   = [[Franceville]], Gabon
|attendance = 
|referee    = Aklesso Gnama ([[Togolese Football Federation|Togo]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021058
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
| date       = 27 March
| time       = {{UTZ|14:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|CHA}}
| score      = 0–4
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/chad-vs-burundi/4048693
| team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Jordi Liongola|Liongola]] {{goal|6}}
*[[Bienvenue Kanakimana|Kanakimana]] {{goal|32}}
*[[Abdoul Karim Mpawenimana|Mpawenimana]] {{goal|63}}
*[[Jean Claude Girumugisha|Girumugisha]] {{goal|74}}
| stadium    = [[:fr:Stade Olympique Maréchal Idriss Déby Itno|Stade Olympique Maréchal Idriss Déby Itno]]
| location   = [[N'Djamena]], Chad
| attendance =
| referee    = Ailton Carmelino ([[Angolan Football Federation|Angola]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
| date       = 31 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
| score      = 4–0
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/burundi-vs-chad/4048694
| team2      = {{fb|CHA}}
| goals1     =
*[[Bienvenue Kanakimana|Kanakimana]] {{goal|14||22}}
*[[Jean Claude Girumugisha|Girumugisha]] {{goal|71}}
*[[Abdoul Karim Mpawenimana|Mpawenimana]] {{goal|80}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Intwari Stadium]]
| location   = [[Bujumbura]], Burundi
| attendance =
| referee    = [[Jalal Jayed]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date       = 26 May
|time       = {{UTZ|18:00|+1}}
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score      = 5–0
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/cesdwwnxbc5fmajgroc0hqzy2/dezv8l0fzgcxtejl0dwmy1gyc/2m1wojm5bt709wu4kugtytxqs/b2jg5tj46orgxz0l5il8xz8d0
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Ayoub El Kaabi|El Kaabi]] {{goal|59||63}}
*[[Tawfik Bentayeb|Bentayeb]] {{goal|71}}
*[[Soufiane Benjdida|Benjdida]] {{goal|80||90}}
|team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Mohammed VI Football Academy|Mohammed VI Football Complexe]]
|location   = [[Salé]], Morocco
|attendance = 0 ([[Behind closed doors (sport)|BCD]])
|referee    = 
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|ZAM}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Burundi
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ZAM}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|BDI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Zambia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Burundi
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|BDI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Togo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ALG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|BDI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Algeria
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|ALG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Burundi
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
| Head Coach
|{{flagicon|DRC}} [[Patrick Sangwa Mayani]]
|-
| Assistant Coach
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Philippe Habimana
|-
| Goalkeeping Coach
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Abdoul Ndizeye
|-
| Match Analyst
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Auguste Nduwimana
|-
| Fitness Coach
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Antoine Nshimiyimana
|-
| Doctors
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Dr. Gauthier Ndayambaje<br>{{flagicon|BDI}} Dr. Laurent Ngabonziza
|-
| Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|BDI}} Baudouin Niyomukiza<br>{{flagicon|BDI}} Gervayi Ngendahayo<br>{{flagicon|BDI}} André Ndayishimiye<br>{{flagicon|BDI}} Flavien Niyigena
|-
| Team Coordinator 
|{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Selemani Ndikumana]]
|-
| Technical Director
|{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Olivier Niyungeko]]
|}

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|Soviet Union}} [[:ru:Ефимов, Николай Фадеевич|Nikolay Yefimov]] (Until 1991)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Baudouin Ribakare]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|RUS}} [[Aleksandr Rakitsky]] (1993–1996)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Baudouin Ribakare]] (1997–2004)
*{{flagicon|ALG}} [[Adel Amrouche]] (2007–2012)
*{{flagicon|EGY}} [[Lofty Naseem]] (2012–2014)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Rainer Willfeld]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|ALG}} [[Ahcene Aït-Abdelmalek]] (2015–2016)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Olivier Niyungeko]] (2016–2019)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Joslin Bipfubusa]] (2020)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Jimmy Ndayizeye]] (2020–2022)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Etienne Ndayiragije]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|DRC}} [[Patrick Sangwa Mayani]] (2024-present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up to the squad for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly]] matches against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] and [[Equatorial Guinea national football team|Equatorial Guinea]] on 26 May and 4 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/IntambaZacu/posts/pfbid02oChCfANFK7zuXCK8fV2cFHRuWs2E4WXJP8ftEPSHEFDJHQB9EiF42UEg6M67H5qql|title=Urutonde rw’abakinyi b’umugwi|publisher=[[Football Federation of Burundi]]|date=19 May 2026|via=[[Facebook]]}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 29 December 2024, after the match against [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Jonathan Nahimana]]|age={{Bda|1999|12|12|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=0|club=[[Vision F.C. (Burundi)|Vision]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Onésime Rukundo]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|4|9|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Daniel Hakizimana]]|age={{Bda|2001|4|25|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Vision FC|Vision]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kevin Icoyitungiye]]|age={{Bda|2004|11|21|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Omar Moussa (footballer)|Omar Moussa]]|age={{Bda|1997|8|30|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Gorilla F.C. (Rwanda)|Gorilla]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Claus Niyukuri]]|age={{Bda|2000|2|13|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FK Haugesund|Haugesund]]|clubnat=NOR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Christophe Nduwarugira]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|6|22|df=y}}|caps=48|goals=6|club=[[Borneo F.C. Samarinda|Borneo Samarinda]]|clubnat=IDN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Akbar Muderi]]|age={{Bda|2003|4|28|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[Gasogi United]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Derrick Mukombozi]]|age={{Bda|1999|4|26|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Fountain Gate F.C.|Fountain Gate]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Djuma Nzeyimana]]|age={{Bda|2000|1|1|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Youssouf Ndayishimiye]]|age={{Bda|1998|10|27|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=1|club=[[OGC Nice|Nice]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Saidi Ntibazonkiza]]|age={{Bda|1987|5|1|df=y}}|caps=31|goals=13|club=[[Vital'O FC|Vital'O]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Aaron Musore]]|age={{Bda|1999|12|28|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Green Eagles F.C.|Green Eagles]]|clubnat=ZAM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Henry Msanga]]|age={{Bda|2001|8|24|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdoul Karim Mpawenimana]]|age={{Bda|2002|10|22|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=2|club=[[CD Costa do Sol|Costa do Sol]]|clubnat=MOZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Leonard Gakwaya]]|age={{Bda|2002|6|1|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mossi Nduwumwe]]|age={{Bda|2003|11|29|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Singida Black Stars FC|Singida Black Stars]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Emanuel Swedi]]|age={{Bda|2003|12|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Nordic United FC|Nordic United]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Cédric Amissi]]|age={{Birth date and age|1990|3|20|df=y}}|caps=55|goals=10|club=[[AS Inter Star|Inter Star]]|clubnat=BDI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jordi Liongola]]|age={{Bda|2000|5|17|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[RAAL La Louvière|La Louvière]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bonfils-Caleb Bimenyimana]]|age={{Birth date and age|1997|11|21|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=5|club=[[Jeddah Club|Jeddah]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bienvenue Kanakimana]]|age={{Bda|1999|12|28|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=3|club=[[FC Zbrojovka Brno|Zbrojovka Brno]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jean-Claude Girumugisha]]|age={{Birth date and age|2004|9|18|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Al Hilal Club (Omdurman)|Al Hilal]]|clubnat=SUD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Vancy Mabanza]]|age={{Bda|2000|11|27|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[K.S.C. Lokeren (2025)|Lokeren]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Franck Nduwimana]]|age={{Bda|2002|4|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Gorilla F.C. (Rwanda)|Gorilla]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Burundi in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=16|pos=GK|name=[[Mattéo Nkurunziza]]|age={{Bda|2004|6|2|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[R.W.D. Molenbeek (2015)|RWD Molenbeek]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Aladin Bizimana]]|age={{Bda|2000|4|1|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Royale Union Saint-Gilloise|Union SG U23]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|GAM}}; 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Dieudonné Ntibahezwa]]|age={{Bda|1998|2|2|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Flambeau du Centre]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=19|pos=DF|name=[[Frédéric Nsabiyumva]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{Bda|1995|4|26|df=y}}|caps=48|goals=1|club=[[Västerås SK Fotboll|Västerås]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Keita Bukuru]]|age={{Bda|2005|4|5|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Marco Weymans]]|age={{Birth date and age|1997|7|9|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[K Beerschot VA|Beerschot]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Olivier Dushime]]|age={{Bda|2002|6|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Flambeau du Centre]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Moussa Muryango]]|age={{Bda|1999|5|25|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Vision F.C. (Burundi)|Vision]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Patrick Mutimana]]|age={{Bda|2000|6|17|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Rukinzo FC|Rukinzo]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nicolas Iragi]]|age={{Bda|1995|11|26|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=21|pos=MF|name=[[Abedi Bigirimana]]|age={{Bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=4|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Elie Mokono]]|age={{Bda|1994|2|23|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Singida Fountain Gate F.C.|Singida Fountain Gate]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Parfait Bizoza]]|age={{Bda|1999|3|3|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FK Haugesund|Haugesund]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Shassiri Nahimana]]|age={{Bda|1993|8|5|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=2|club=[[Pamba S.C.|Pamba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gaël Bigirimana]]|age={{Bda|1993|10|22|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Dungannon Swifts F.C.|Dungannon Swifts]]|clubnat=NIR|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jospin Nshimirimana]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|12|12|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=8|club=[[Cape Town City F.C. (2016)|Cape Town City]]|clubnat=RSA|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Karim Kamana]]|age={{Bda|2004|1|3|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Edson Munaba]]|age={{Bda|2003|12|20|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Al-Madina SC|Al-Madina]]|clubnat=LBY|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Richard Ndayishimiye]]|age={{Bda|2004|1|1|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Trésor Mossi]]|age={{Bda|2001|8|28|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Sumgayit FK|Sumgayit]]|clubnat=AZE|latest=v. {{fb|GAM}}; 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ismail Nshimirimana]]|age={{Bda|2000|1|1|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Diedrick Bubahe]]|age={{Bda|2005|7|30|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Brattvåg IL|Brattvåg]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jean Nahimana]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Aigle Noir Makamba FC|Aigle Noir]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Asman Ndikumana]]|age={{Bda|1999|6|10|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=2|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Abdi Ineza]]|age={{Bda|2004|12|12|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Ghazl El Mahalla SC|Ghazl El Mahalla]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|CHA}}; 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Richard Kirongozi]]|age={{Bda|2000|6|13|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}; 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Sudi Abdallah]]|age={{Bda|2000|1|5|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=3|club=[[Persijap Jepara]]|clubnat=IDN|latest=v. {{fb|GAM}}; 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mohamed Amissi]]|age={{Bda|2000|8|3|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[Modern Sport FC|Modern Sport]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Saido Berahino]]|age={{Bda|1993|8|4|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=2|club=[[NK Tabor Sežana|Tabor Sežana]]|clubnat=SVN|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dan Ntiharirizwa]]|age={{Bda|2006|2|12|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Emmen|Emmen]]|clubnat=NED|latest=v. {{fb|MTN}}; 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br/>
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br/>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br/>
<sup>WIT</sup> Withdrew from the squad
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team, red or yellow cards.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|14 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Burundi |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/33/Burundi.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Burundi.''

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
|+Most appearances
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Karim Nizigiyimana]] || 66 || 0 || 2004–2023
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Cédric Amissi]] || 56 || 10 || 2009–2023
|-
| 3 ||align=left| '''[[Christophe Nduwarugira]]''' || 54 || 7 || 2012–present
|-
| 4 ||align=left| '''[[Fiston Abdul Razak]]''' || 53 || 19 || 2009–present
|-
| 5 ||align=left| '''[[Frédéric Nsabiyumva]]''' || 52 || 1 || 2013–present
|-
| 6 ||align=left| '''[[Shassiri Nahimana]]''' || 51 || 2 || 2013–present
|-
| 7 ||align=left| [[Gaël Duhayindavyi]] || 50 || 2 || 2011–2019
|-
| rowspan=3|8 ||align=left| [[Hassan Hakizimana]] || 46 || 1 || 2007–2014
|-
|align=left| [[Pierre Kwizera]] || 46 || 3 || 2009–2019
|-
|align=left| '''[[Jonathan Nahimana]]''' || 46 || 0 || 2017–present
|-
|}

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
|+Top goalscorers
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| '''[[Fiston Abdul Razak]]''' || 19 || 53 || {{#expr:19/53 round 2}} || 2009–present
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Saidi Ntibazonkiza]]''' || 13 || 36 || {{#expr:13/36 round 2}} || 2004–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Selemani Ndikumana]] || 12 || 33 || {{#expr:12/33 round 2}} || 2003–2019
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Banga Lewis Kubi]] || 11 || 16 || {{#expr:11/16 round 2}} || 1998–2004
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Cédric Amissi]] || 10 || 56 || {{#expr:10/56 round 2}} || 2009–2023
|-
| rowspan=2|6 ||align=left| '''[[Jospin Nshimirimana]]''' || 8 || 15 || {{#expr:8/15 round 2}} || 2020–present
|-
|align=left| [[Papa Claude Nahimana]] || 8 || 29 || {{#expr:8/29 round 2}} || 2007–2013
|-
| rowspan=2|8 ||align=left| '''[[Bonfils-Caleb Bimenyimana]]''' || 7 || 27 || {{#expr:7/27 round 2}} || 2017–present
|-
|align=left| '''[[Christophe Nduwarugira]]''' || 7 || 54 || {{#expr:7/54 round 2}} || 2012–present
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Amissi Tambwe]] || 6 || 25 || {{#expr:6/25 round 2}} || 2011–2020
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Belgium}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|Belgium}}''
|-
| [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] and [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]] to [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8 |''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 |''Declined participation''
|-
| {{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|4
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|2
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|2
|0
|4
|5
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4
|2
|0
|2
|5
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|2
|0
|2
|0
|2
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|3
|1
|6
|13
|13
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/13|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!32
!10
!6
!16
!32
!41
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{Main|Burundi at the Africa Cup of Nations}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]] to {{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Belgium}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]] to {{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]||colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Cup of Nations|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]] to {{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]] to {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
||Group stage||22nd||3||0||0||3||0||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total||Group stage||1/35||3||0||0||3||0||4
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 1
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||rowspan=2 colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||Group stage|| 10th||3||1||1||1||4||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||rowspan=5 colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!1/8
!3
!1
!1
!1
!4
!4
|}

===CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[CECAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2004 CECAFA Cup|2004]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
* [http://ffb.bi/ Burundi Football Federation] {{in lang|fr}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/bdi/ Burundi] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/BDI Burundi] at [[FIFA]]

{{Football in Burundi}}
{{CECAFA Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Burundi}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Burundi National Football Team}}
[[Category:Burundi national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1964]]
